questions.question: Describe the condition that must be met for two or more processes to be in a deadlocked state, as per the Banks' Concurrency Algorithm. (5 marks)
Explain the concept of a 'no preemption' scheduling policy and how it relates to deadlock detection and prevention in operating systems. (10 marks)

A system has two processes, P1 and P2, which are deadlocked. P1 holds the file F1 and is waiting for F2, while P2 holds the file F2 and is waiting for F1. What is the type of deadlock that has occurred? (5 marks)
